contentBlocks: Student Success: We define success as an ability to think critically, grow professionally, achieve goals, and contribute to the community.

Vision: We strive to be a valued community of scholars in which students are engaged in diverse learning experiences with faculty and staff who are devoted to student success and technical achievement.

Mission: Through contemporary curricula, engaging pedagogy, scholarship and applied research, we produce career-ready graduates prepared for a lifetime of professional achievement and intellectual growth.
Values: We value student success, academic excellence, scholarship, innovation, a balance of theory and practice, diversity, opportunity, community engagement, integrity, and accountability.

links.text: Order of the Engineer Ring Ceremony

pages.title: Order of the Engineer | Sacramento State
pages.description: The Order of the Engineer is an association for graduate and professional engineers in the United States that emphasizes pride and responsibility in the engineering profession.
headings: College of Engineering & Computer Science California State University, Sacramento
contentBlocks: The Order of the Engineer Ring Ceremony is a tradition honoring graduating engineering students as they take the "Obligation of an Engineer"—a lifelong pledge to uphold ethical standards and serve humanity through responsible engineering practices. Participants will receive a stainless steel ring, worn on the fifth finger of the working hand, symbolizing their commitment to integrity and professionalism.
Date: Tuesday, May 19, 2026 Time: 4:30 PM – 5:30 PM Location: University Union, Pacific Suite (3rd Floor)

The Order of the Engineer is a distinguished tradition that recognizes graduating engineering students as they take the "Obligation of an Engineer"—a public pledge to uphold the highest standards of ethics, integrity, and professionalism in their work. During the Ring Ceremony, candidates are formally inducted into the Order and receive a stainless steel ring worn on the fifth finger of the working hand. This ring symbolizes their lifelong commitment to the engineering profession and its ethical responsibilities. Inspired by the Hippocratic Oath taken by medical professionals, the Obligation of an Engineer reflects the core values found in the codes of ethics of major engineering societies. It serves as a solemn promise to protect the public, uphold the dignity of the profession, and responsibly steward Earth’s resources. The Order is not a traditional membership organization—there are no meetings or dues. Instead, it serves as a symbolic commitment, uniting engineers in a shared purpose to serve humanity with integrity and skill.
The ring signifies that you have publicly vowed to practice integrity, fairness and respect in all aspects of engineering. The ring shows you will practice the qualities of a professional engineer.
Sign Up & Ring Sizing: Visit the ECS Dean’s Office (2014 Riverside Hall).
Payment: Submit a $15 fee at the Bursar’s Office or online using the ring purchase payment form .
Ceremony: Attend the Ring Induction to receive your ring and certificate.

pages.title: Senior Project Showcase | Sacramento State
pages.description: ECS - Senior Project Showcase. Graduating seniors in Civil Engineering, Computer Engineering, Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, and Mechanical Engineering will present highlights of their senior projects.
headings: College of Engineering & Computer Science California State University, Sacramento
Computer Science - CSC 190/191: Senior Project I/II
Electrical & Electronic Engineering - EEE 192A:Electrical Power Design I
Electrical & Electronic Engineering-EEE 192B:Electrical Power Design II
EEE 193A / Computer Engineering - CPE 190: Senior Design Project I
EEE 193B / Computer Engineering - CPE 191: Product Design Project II
Mechanical Engineering - ME 190: Project Engineering I
Mechanical Engineering - ME 191: Project Engineering II
contentBlocks: Spring 2026 Senior Project Showcase 📅 Date: Friday, May 15, 2026 🕗 Time: 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM Join us for the Spring 2026 Senior Project Showcase, where graduating seniors from Civil Engineering, Computer Engineering, Computer Science, Electrical & Electronic Engineering, and Mechanical Engineering will present their final projects. Students collaborate in small groups, partnering with private companies, public agencies, or developing independent, innovative projects. The event will feature both presentations and product demonstrations, highlighting the creativity and technical skills of our future engineers and computer scientists. Don't miss this celebration of innovation and achievement!

If you are interested in supporting the College of Engineering & Computer Science, your contribution can make a lasting impact on our students, faculty, and programs. From scholarships and lab enhancements to student project sponsorship and industry partnerships, there are many ways to give.
Explore giving opportunities and learn how you can support ECS at: Ways to Give and Support the College.

If you are an ECS student, your senior project class is participating in SPS, and you need to have an SPS project poster printed and mounted, please read and follow the instructions on the SPS Poster Upload & Tracking Page

Student teams work on the development of a software application for a public or private sector client. Team members must collaborate with each other as well as with their client to ensure the software delivered provides the value that the client expects. Second semester of a two semester senior project course.

Student teams plan, research, and design aspects of energy systems, including generation, transmission, and distribution systems. Emphasis is placed on design philosophies, problem definition, research, project planning, communication skills, teamwork, development of specifications, and effective utilization of available resources.
Place: Tschannen Engineering Building Breezeway
Also Guy West Plaza and Riverside Hall Courtyard - weather permitting.

Centers on developing hardware and software project planning and engineering design skills. Emphasis is placed on design philosophies, problem definition, project planning and budgeting, written and oral communication skills, working with others in a team arrangement, development of specifications and effective utilization of available resources.

Student teams design and implement projects started in EEE 193A/CPE 190. The hardware is designed, tested, and redesigned if necessary. Required software is written, debugged, and incorporated into a written report. The final results of the project are presented.
Student teams design a product, device, or apparatus that will be fabricated in ME 191. Students work in small groups and interact with product users, vendors, and faculty advisors.
Continuation of the project started in ME 190. Teams fabricate, assemble, test, evaluate, and report on their product, device, or apparatus.

Completion of a conceptual design and evaluation of alternatives under realistic constraints for proposed infrastructure projects. Students work in teams with practicing professionals providing mentoring .
Place: University Union - Hinde Auditorium

Engineering study skills, fundamentals of the engineering design process and problem solving, current engineering challenges. Distinctions and similarities of different engineering disciplines. Students learn about the engineering profession, ethics, teamwork, & communication skills. Projects incorporating all these elements will be displayed by student teams.

For more information about the ECS Senior Project Showcase or for inquiries about participation in future showcases as a project sponsor, client, or technical mentor, please contact Dean Kevan Shafizadeh at kevan@csus.edu .

pages.title: ECS Inclusive Belonging & Engagement Committee (IBEC) | Sacramento State
pages.description: Statement by the ECS Inclusive Belonging & Engagement Committee (IBEC)
headings: About The College College of Engineering & Computer Science
contentBlocks: The College of Engineering and Computer Science stands committed to and is dedicated to fostering a community that values and celebrates diversity, inclusive belonging, and engagement. We recognize that our strength lies in our differences and strive to create an environment where everyone feels valued, respected, and supported. We consider diversity and inclusion to be key drivers of innovation and creativity, which are essential in our fields. We are committed to promoting an inclusive culture that celebrates the diversity of perspectives, backgrounds, and experiences of our students, faculty, and staff. We strive to eliminate all forms of discrimination, bias, and prejudice, and to create a sense of belonging for all members of our community. We foster equity in all aspects of our day-to-day operations and planning, including hiring, curricular development, and retention. Sacramento State University is both a Hispanic Serving Institution (HSI) and an Asian American and Native American Pacific Islander Serving Institute (AANAPISI). We provide educational opportunities and resources to help all members of our community understand and appreciate the value of diversity, inclusive belonging, and engagement. Our goal is to create a supportive community that encourages the expression of individuality and promotes the growth and development of all members. We work together to eliminate barriers, encourage dialogue, and foster a sense of belonging and community in our programs. As a college, we are devoted to making a positive impact on the world through our teaching, research and scholarly activities, as well as university and community service efforts. By fostering a culture of inclusive belonging and engagement, we believe we can create a brighter future for our campus and community. Together, we will continue to build an inclusive, equitable, and respectful culture, where everyone has the opportunity to achieve their dreams and make a positive impact on the Sacramento region and the broader global community.

contentBlocks: We define student success as the ability to think critically, grow professionally, achieve goals, and contribute to the community. To advance student success, our staff engage students with a personal interest in student well-being. Our faculty advance student success through the delivery of outstanding academic programming, meaningful applied research, and impactful community service. As a result, we are proud to be recognized as a top employer destination for engineering, computer science and construction management leaders.

pages.description: Information for students and employers about ECS Internships & Career Services. Co-ops, site-agreements, ECS Internship & Career Fair, Resume help, job listings via Handshake, interview help, employer info sessions.
headings: Internships & Career Services College of Engineering & Computer Science
Connect with ECS Internship & Career Services
contentBlocks: The College of Engineering and Computer Science (ECS) Internship & Career Services Office offers the following services to ECS students:
Career Fairs, Info Sessions, and Workshops: Get connected with employers through the various events we host throughout the year.

Find Jobs and Internships: Handshake provides students with the opportunity to seek full time jobs, internships, both on and off-campus jobs, explore various events like career fairs, informational sessions, tabling events, and workshops. Additionally, it facilitates networking opportunities with both employers and fellow students. Use your MySacState credentials to log in and set up your profile. For help getting started, visit the Handshake Support Page or contact the ECS Internship & Career Services Log in to Handshake

Make an appointment with a Career Counselor: M ake an appointment with a Career Counselor for help with resume, cover letter, interview coaching, job and internship search, networking and salary negotiation Make an Appointment
Internships/Co-op Information for Employers
The College of Engineering and Computer Science (ECS) offers the following services to employers.
Online Job Listings: Our partnership with Handshake allows companies to post jobs and Internships on Handshake .

Annual Internship & Career Fairs: The College of ECS hosts Internship & Career Fairs every spring and fall . Typically 100+ companies participate, providing candidates with internship, career, and corporate information.
Info Sessions: Employers can host info sessions, workshops, seminars, and interviews. Contact ecscareer@csus.edu to schedule.
Co-op Services: Employers can inquire about hiring upper division and graduate students for 6-month cooperative education learning experiences (these are generally full-time paid positions).
Partnership Program: Become a partner, unlock exclusive benefits, and elevate your brand! Contact ecscareer@csus.edu with any questions.
Learn More About Our Partnership Program

pages.title: ECS CCATS | Sacramento State
pages.description: ECS Computing, Communications & Academic Technology Services (CCATS). ECS student & faculty/staff IT support. Networking, hardware, software & helpdesk.
headings: ECS CCATS College of Engineering & Computer Science
ECS Computing, Communications & Academic Technology Services (CCATS)
contentBlocks: The Computing, Communications & Academic Technology Services (CCATS) unit is the integrated Information and Academic Technology (IT/AT) unit for the College of ECS. It supports hardware, software, networking, and consulting for both academic and research computing and communications. The organization has evolved significantly over the years and today is focused on supporting a highly distributed and virtualized computing and communications environment.
Create a Service Ticket to Get Help from CCATS

If you are an ECS student, faculty, or staff you can self-enroll to the ECS Resource Center Canvas course . The course has detailed HOW-TOs, announcements, and useful articles specifically geared towards ECS students, faculty, and staff. This course is available to all ECS users and has articles explaining everything from VPN access and ECS Linux servers to ECS key fobs for after-hours access.
Self-Enroll to the ECS Resource Center Canvas Course

pages.title: Hornet Leadership Program | Sacramento State
pages.description: STEM Peer Hornet Leadership Program
headings: Hornet Leadership Program College of Engineering & Computer Science
contentBlocks: The Hornet Leadership Program (HLP) empowers ECS students to stand out by building the leadership and career-ready skills employers value most. Through hands-on experiences beyond the classroom, students develop strong communication, teamwork, problem-solving, and professional leadership skills.

Each semester, participants take part in engaging leadership seminars/workshops, while completing leadership service hours that turn learning into action. These experiences provide real-world opportunities to collaborate with peers and mentors, connect directly with industry professionals, and gain practical skills that translate into career success.

HLP prepares students for competitive careers and graduate programs by fostering confidence, initiative, and professional growth. The program inspires students to lead with purpose, make meaningful connections, and create a lasting impact within ECS and beyond.
Hornet Leadership Program Riverside Hall, Room 2014 Mon. – Fri. 8 a.m. – 5 p.m.

pages.title: Employment Opportunities | Sacramento State
pages.description: Find employment opportunities at Sac State's College of Engineering and Computer Science.
headings: College of Engineering & Computer Science California State University, Sacramento
contentBlocks: The College has an on-going need to supplement its current pool of part-time lecturers.

A Master’s degree in the relevant field is required, but a doctoral degree in the field is preferred. Excellent communication skills are required. Teaching experience at the university level is highly desirable but not required. Professional engineering experience and board registration are desirable. Compensation is variable, depending upon qualifications and relevant experience.

The anticipated need for part-time lecturers is ongoing, and there is no deadline for applications. Part-time instructor positions are typically filled between October and January for the spring semester and between March and August for the fall semester.

pages.title: Opportunities for Engagement | Sacramento State
pages.description: Opportunities for engagement with the College of ECS. Guidance for individuals, corporations, foundations, and other organizations to become more involved.
headings: About The College College of Engineering & Computer Science
Mentor a Student Organization or Competitive Team
Support or Mentor a Senior Design Team
Sponsor an ECS Student Ambassador or Student Assistant
contentBlocks: The College of Engineering & Computer Science maintains strong ties with its alumni, donors, and friends within the greater engineering, computer science, and construction management communities. There are many ways that individuals, corporations, foundations, and other organizations can become more involved with our students, faculty, and staff and the programs that support them.
Alumni, if you have a story idea or information to share, we want to hear about it!

Tell us about life or career accomplishments since graduation from Sacramento State, and include a photo if you have one, and you may find yourself in an alumni newsletter or annual report. Contact the ECS Dean’s Office at (916) 278-6366.

Fall Welcomes and “Evenings With Industry” - Every fall, our departments welcome students back by hosting events that offer a unique interaction between students and representatives from various industries. Panelists from all sectors of the professional community discuss recent projects, and answer student questions about career opportunities, industry trends, and life after graduation.

Senior Project Showcase - Each semester, the College hosts an event that showcases senior design projects including hardware demonstrations, and poster and PowerPoint presentations. This showcase is free, and open to the public and members of professional community.
If you want to gain recognition of your company by students or faculty/staff, you can sponsor a College event, such as:
Annual Student “Welcome Back” BBQ or Spring “Resource Fair.”
Annual College Scholarship Reception (particularly nice if you already support or want to support a scholarship).
National “ECS Week,” where we host a series of events for our students and local K-12 schools with potential “future Hornets.”
Discussion, Discovery, Dissemination (“D3”) Reception, where faculty talk about their scholarly interests with potential collaborators.
ECS Joint Council (ECSJC) Community Exposition, which is our annual student-run exposition of student projects and competition teams.
New Faculty Orientation, where we provide support to new full-time and part-time faculty.
You could also sponsor the ECS student t-shirts that are given to new students or ECS polos that are given to full-time faculty and staff each year.

ECS Internship and Career Services Office hosts career fairs in the fall and spring, bringing together firms and agencies seeking to improve or expand their workforce with our students. The ECS Internship and Career Services Office can also facilitate information sessions or help conduct oncampus interviews throughout the year. For more info, visit the ECS Internship & Career Center .

Seminars - Guest speakers are sought for introductory seminar courses to introduce students to different disciplines and career opportunities within the engineering and computer science fields. Student Clubs - The College has active professional student organizations that seek industry guest speakers or localproject tour guides. These student organizations also have teams that welcome guidance on activities related to annual student competitions. A list of clubs is found at www.ecs.csus.edu/clubs .

The College and its departments meet with active members in the local engineering, computer science, and construction management communities who provide guidance and recommendations to our programs and to help organize industry events. Please email the Dean’s office if interested in joining a committee: ecs-doassist@csus.edu
Many of our professional student organizations compete against other universities, and they seek coaching and mentorship from industry leaders.

Every semester, our senior design courses seek project suggestions as well as involvement by practicing, professionals who serve as either “technical mentors” or “clients” to provide guidance or evaluate completed student projects. Please email the Dean’s office if interested: ecs-doassist@csus.edu

Our faculty and graduate students welcome partnerships with practicing engineers to work on industry-inspired and sponsored research projects. The College can serve as a resource for the local professional community.

Our departments are always looking for qualified and enthusiastic part-time instructors who can help teach courses that meet the demands of our growing student population. Teaching experience is not required, but there are academic and professional requirements.

Support our undergraduate or graduate students in the form of financial support. All gifts are tax deductible. More information about scholarships is available by contacting the College at (916) 278-6366.
You can support the College through an equipment or materials donation or an in-kind gift. All donations can be arranged by contacting the College at (916) 278-6366.
The easiest way to make a gift is online through the official College giving page. Other gifts can be arranged by contacting the College at (916) 278-6366.

Available to all College of Engineering and Computer Science students at Sacramento State, the Hornet Leadership Program (HLP) is an extra-curricular program with a goal to produce college graduates who are prepared for leadership in their career field. Topics include critical thinking, conflict resolution, strategic planning, communication (verbal, written), business etiquette, Robert’s Rules, budgets and contracts, intellectual property, and ethics in the profession.
The Hornet Leadership Program is self-sustained through the generous support from our industry partners. The culminating experience of the program is a student-led community engagement project.

The College has launched a new outreach and recruitment program to employ our students to give campus tours of ECS labs and community outreach to visit local high schools. This program is focused on showcasing our existing labs to the diverse population of middle and high school students, as well as community college students, who visit the Sac State campus to learn more about being a “Future Hornet.”

Your sponsorship will support the training and materials necessary to effectively represent the college to the general public. There are also opportunities to sponsor student employees who assist our college equipment technicians in our MADLab or our college IT staff.

Our focus is on educating the leaders of tomorrow. We do not always have the resources to produce communications/marketing materials for our wide audience of stakeholders which include current and prospective students and their families, current and prospective faculty/staff, or our alumni and industry stakeholders.

Let’s work together to produce promotional materials, such as semesterly newsletters, promotional “one- pagers,” short videos, photography services, and social media posts that we can like and share with others. The list of ways you or your organization could help is long. Let’s talk about how you can help make a difference.
Mentor a Student Organization or Competitive Team
Sponsor a Collaborative Research Project
Sponsor an ECS Student Ambassador or Student Assistant
Donate Gifts in Kind: Communications/Marketing

pages.title: Computer Engineering | Sacramento State
pages.description: Computer Engineering Program at Sac State. With its roots in programming, software & electrical design the CPE Program teaches computer architecture, logic design, digital signal processing & networking.
headings: Computer Engineering Program College of Engineering & Computer Science
contentBlocks: Computer Engineering has some of its roots in programming languages, software design, and operating systems from Computer Science, it also has roots from Electrical Engineering, namely circuit theory, electronics, and hardware design. Computer Engineering is particularly strong in the areas of computer architecture, computer hardware, logic design, digital signal processing and networking.

Sacramento State was one of the early Universities to offer a BS in Computer Engineering degree in the State of California. Today, most Engineering Universities in the USA offer such a degree in some fashion. At Sac State, this degree is offered through a joint program sponsored by the Computer Science Department and the Electrical & Electronic Engineering Department. This arrangement has the advantage of support from two strong departments.
The Bachelor of Science degree in Computer Engineering is accredited by the Computing Accreditation Commission of Accreditation Board of Engineering and Technology.
Fri, May 15 Spring 2026 Last Day of Instruction
Mon, May 18 - Fri, May 22 Spring 2026 Finals Week
Computer Engineering Riverside Hall, Room 3022 Mondays and Wednesdays, 9 a.m. – 12 p.m.

pages.title: Computer Science | Sacramento State
pages.description: The Computer Science program at Sac State is one part of its College of Engineering & Computer Science.
headings: Department of Computer Science College of Engineering & Computer Science
contentBlocks: Sacramento State's Computer Science Department is one of the oldest and largest computer science programs in the CSU system.

Having educated computer professionals since 1969, our programs currently serve approximately 1600 major students. The Department also offers a variety of non-major courses to students who seek practical experience in the application of information technology, enabling individuals with a background in other areas to obtain the skills and knowledge necessary to enter or advance in computer-related industries.

The Bachelor of Science in Computer Science program provides majors with a sound educational base. The Master of Science prepares students for research, teaching, or further study towards the Ph.D. in Computer Science or Software Engineering.
Center for Information Assurance & Security
Faculty Advising Hours (Current ECS Students Only)
The Bachelor of Science degree in Computer Science is accredited by the Computing Accreditation Commission of Accreditation Board of Engineering and Technology.

pages.title: Department of Mechanical Engineering | Sacramento State
pages.description: The Mechanical Engineering program at Sac State is one part of its College of Engineering & Computer Science.
headings: Department of Mechanical Engineering College of Engineering & Computer Science
contentBlocks: Mechanical Engineering applies the principles of science and mathematics to the design of machinery and equipment used in the rapidly evolving areas of transportation, manufacturing and energy conversion. Balancing fundamentals, analytical methods, and design applications, the program prepares graduates for long-term engineering careers.
The Mechanical Engineering Department offers the following degrees:
Bachelor of Science (BS) Degree in Mechanical Engineering
Master of Science (MS) Degree in Mechanical Engineering

For Mechanical Engineering undergraduate students seeking to further their studies in achieving a Master's Degree in Mechanical Engineering, the Department offers an accelerated dual-degree Bachelor's/Master's program.
2025-2026 Part-Time Lecturer Application
The BS ME program is accredited by the Engineering Accreditation Commission of ABET .

pages.title: Civil Engineering | Sacramento State
pages.description: The Civil Engineering program at Sac State is one part of its College of Engineering & Computer Science.
headings: Department of Civil Engineering College of Engineering & Computer Science
contentBlocks: Civil engineers are instrumental in planning, analyzing, and designing the infrastructure we take for granted in rural and urban areas. The undergraduate program at Sacramento State leads to a Bachelor of Science degree and is accredited by the Engineering Accreditation Commission (EAC) of ABET, https://www.abet.org/.

Graduates are prepared to enter the profession as practicing civil engineers with a focus on applied engineering principles. The department also offers advanced study and concentrations in multiples areas of civil engineering in the Master of Science degree program.
Why Civil Engineering at Sacramento State?
Opportunities for Alumni and Practicing Engineers

An Evening with Industry (under construction)

pages.title: Department of Electrical & Electronic Engineering | Sacramento State
pages.description: The Electrical Engineering program at Sac State is one part of its College of Engineering & Computer Science.
headings: Department of Electrical & Electronic Engineering College of Engineering & Computer Science
About Department of Electrical & Electronic Engineering
contentBlocks: Our program’s strengths lie in its curriculum and outstanding laboratory facilities. The EEE curriculum focuses on hands-on experience and provides a strong theoretical and practical background for students to enter the workforce and thrive in their chosen career. Our alumni are hired by local and nationwide industry-leading companies.
Bachelor of Science (BS) Degree in Electrical and Electronic Engineering
Master of Science (MS) Degree in Electrical and Electronic Engineering

The B.S. degrees in Electrical and Electronic Engineering and Computer Engineering are accredited by the Engineering Accreditation Commission of the Accreditation Board of Engineering and Technology (ABET).
EEE Graduate Certificate in Mixed-Signal Design
Fri, May 15 Spring 2026 Last Day of Instruction
Mon, May 18 - Fri, May 22 Spring 2026 Finals Week
Electrical Engineering Riverside Hall, Room 3018 Mon–Fri 8a.m. – 5p.m., closed 12pm – 1pm

pages.title: ECS Helpdesk Remote Tools | Sacramento State
pages.description: Remote Tools for connecting to ECS resources from home.
headings: ECS CCATS College of Engineering & Computer Science
How do I use ECS Labs Remotely?
Connecting to the CSUS VPN via the Global Protect VPN Client
What's a VPN, why do I need to use it?
Zoom Video Conferencing and Meeting software
contentBlocks: The College of ECS provides access to ECS labs remotely.

The campus also provides Zoom, a web video conferencing solution, to help get into contact with staff, students, or to study together remotely. Please see the tutorials on this page to help you get started.
If you have questions/issues regarding any of these tools please contact the ECS Helpdesk Student Assistants for clarification

Starting Spring 2024, we will be using Microsoft Azure Virtual Desktops (AVD) as our primary way to provide access to ECS software remotely. Direct ECS lab system access via Sassafras will not be available after Spring 2024 so it is important to fully test AVD now.
AVD information and connection instructions for ECS students, faculty, and staff.

Sac State offers Virtual Computer Labs to help you stay connected to the essential tools you need for coursework. You can connect to a Windows-based virtual computer using virtually any device. In addition to the general Sac State AVDs, ECS students can connect to ECS specific AVDs. These ECS AVDs have all ECS engineering software installed. There are two types of AVDs available to ECS students:
These systems have all the lab software that an ECS student would need to compete all ECS-based assignments.

These are intended to be used with software that requires heavy use of a graphics card for rendering or simulation, like Solidworks, Ansys, Autocad, etc. Student access to these systems must be requested by an ECS student or an ECS professor. They shouldn't be used for basic tasks/assignments and only have limited ECS applications installed.
Specific connection Instructions & Access Request (via ECS Resource Center Canvas Course)
ECS Resource Center Canvas Course Information
A VPN is a Virtual Private Network. It creates a secure tunnel from your system to the campus internal network.
Many ECS resources are not available outside of the campus internal network (Linux servers, etc.) so you will need to connect to the campus network using the CSUS VPN .

Zoom is a web conferencing technology which allows the campus community to hold live meetings, webinars, and share content from our screens. All you need is a device with an Internet connection. The Zoom platform offers web conferencing services with both voice and video, and other features like Q&A, polling, remote screen control, whiteboards, and discussions.
All CSUS faculty, staff, and students already have zoom accounts which can be accessed via your normal CSUS Saclink credentials.
You can host video/audio meetings with anyone, not just CSUS faculty/staff/students. People that wish to join meetings do not need the app or a smartphone.
To get started using Zoom, please read the Zoom Instructions or check out Zoom's Video Tutorials .
Connecting to the CSUS VPN via the Global Protect VPN Client
Zoom Video Conferencing and Meeting software

pages.title: Construction Management | Sacramento State
pages.description: Construction Management is a program of the College of Engineering & Computer Science at California State University, Sacramento.
headings: Construction Management Program College of Engineering & Computer Science
contentBlocks: Construction Management is the management of the fascinating and complex process that results in the roads, bridges, buildings and industrial facilities upon which we all depend. The built environment involves marshaling the resources required ― labor, materials, equipment, time and money ― and then translating the work of architects and engineers into tangible structures.

The College of Engineering and Computer Science Student Success Center (SSC) supports students with their academic, career, and professional development. The SSC provides holistic support services through GE and major advising, career advising, and by connecting students to other resources on campus.
The Bachelor of Science degree in Construction Management is accredited by the American Council for Construction Education https://www.acce-hq.org/
Construction Management Program Riverside Hall, Room 4026 Mon – Fri., 8 a.m. – 5 p.m., Closed Noon – 1 p.m.
